rationale: Title Y is more concise, avoids the repetitive phrasing of Title X, and uses stronger clinical outcomes language ('arrhythmia-free survival') that reads more naturally for JICE.
loser_weakness: Title X repeats 'pulmonary vein' three times and 'atrial fibrillation' twice, making it unnecessarily verbose and harder to read despite conveying slightly more specific predictor detail.
